raw_consumer_statement: My elderly mother and I were victims of fraud in Excess of XXXX starting in XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX The XXXX suspects were at our home assisting us, as we are both XXXX. They were at my home 7 days a week, pretending to assist us but it wasnt until XXXX I realized they opened numerous accounts with our information and stole cards from our wallets. The citi double cash card was one card she opened with my knowledge to help us get more available credit she said, so we could furnish our newly done basement. No one was authorized to use my card and no one was supposed to be added as an authorized user. She got the mail from the mailbox everyday and handed me a citi double cash card I think I used 3 times, and I told Citi what transactions where mine. The suspect used the card ( XXXX ) at numerous places and even paid herself and her company with cash advances after she added the card to my XXXX. The detectives and attorney general said this is called account takeover. Both suspects have prior charges for similar activity. Their boss gets them out of jail. He is also being looked at as a suspect. I have told Citi to check cameras, check ip addresses, do whatever they have to but my mother and I did NOT use the card with the exception of the few transactions I informed them about. Our excellent credit rating is ruined and our health has deteriorated because of this traumatic experience and Citi not wanting to investigate this further and not believing us has made it XXXX xs worse. Especially for my mother. The fact that Citi has decided to rule in favor of XXXX known con-artist is extremely disheartening, especially given their records compared to my mothers and mine. The suspect was in control of all the accounts because we naively believed she was looking out for our best interest. Yes I was a fool for trusting so much and I will live with that the rest of my life. But not being believed has made it XXXX Xs worse when I dont know what else to do. The other credit card companies did their investigations and acknowledged the fraud. I dont know why Citi hasnt. Thank you.
